name: Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop
name_original: Villa del Rey desde Mata de Alcántara – Circular por Alcántara y los ríos Tagus y Jartín
description: This moderate 16.9-mile touring cycling route explores the Tagus International Natural Park, featuring rivers and historical sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mata De Alcántara traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, historic towns, and the presence of large reservoirs and natural park areas. The region offers a mix of open countryside and routes that pass through or near traditional villages. Elevations are generally moderate, providing varied terrain suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Mata De Alcántara The most popular touring cycling route is Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop, a 16.9 miles (27.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete, passing through rural landscapes and historic areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is City tour Alcántara – circular by Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional, a difficult 18.5 miles (29.8 km) path. This route features views of the Embalse de José María de Oriol reservoir and sections within the Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional. Local touring cyclists also love the Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through rural villages and open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Mata De Alcántara is defined by historic towns, reservoirs, and natural park areas. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mata De Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Mata De Alcántara?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mata De Alcántara offer a mix of moderate and difficult options. You'll find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop&lt;/a&gt; which is moderate, alongside more challenging paths such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114013055/city-tour-alcantara-circular-by-embalse-de-jose-maria-de-oriol-alcantara-ii-parque-natural-del-tagus-internacional" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour Alcántara – circular by Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mata De Alcántara?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided, routes with moderate difficulty and shorter distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop&lt;/a&gt; (27.2 km), might be suitable for families with some cycling experience. The region's rolling hills mean some elevation changes should be expected.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on a touring bike ride in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Touring cycling routes in Mata De Alcántara traverse a diverse landscape featuring rolling hills, historic towns, and significant natural areas. You'll encounter large reservoirs, open countryside, and routes that pass through or near traditional villages, often with views of the Tagus Internacional Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Mata De Alcántara are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114110581/brozas-and-navas-del-madrono-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Brozas – La Quebrada Lake loop from Brozas&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history and natural beauty. Along your rides, you might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roco-Campofrío Palace&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Almocóvar Church&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the José María de Oriol - Alcántara II Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Mata De Alcántara experiences hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter chill.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of the region's significant water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114013055/city-tour-alcantara-circular-by-embalse-de-jose-maria-de-oriol-alcantara-ii-parque-natural-del-tagus-internacional" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour Alcántara – circular by Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional&lt;/a&gt; specifically features views of the Embalse de José María de Oriol reservoir. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the area.

Q: What are some longer touring cycling options in the area?
A: For a longer ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Brozas – La Quebrada Lake loop from Brozas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 43.2 km. This route offers a substantial touring experience through rural landscapes and past natural features.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near traditional villages and historic towns like Alcántara and Brozas. These settlements typically offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, or small shops where you can rest and refuel during your ride.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while cycling in Mata De Alcántara?
A: The region, particularly areas within the Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional, is known for its diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including raptors, and other local fauna that inhabit the open countryside and natural park areas.

Q: Are there any less-trafficked or 'hidden gem' routes for touring cyclists?
A: While all routes offer a sense of exploration, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Brozas loop from Brozas&lt;/a&gt;, at 31.1 km, offers a pleasant journey through the local countryside and the historic center of Brozas, providing a good option for those looking to explore local charm away from the most popular trails.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Given the region's climate, layered clothing is advisable, especially during spring and autumn mornings. Lightweight, breathable fabrics are best for warmer days, and always carry sun protection. In cooler months, a windproof and waterproof outer layer can be beneficial.
tips: Mata de Alcántara is a small town located within the Tagus River Valley Biosphere Reserve with a beautiful and unique landscape to explore by bicycle. It was created by the Military Order of Alcántara as an important station on the routes to Portugal.

Built in the 16th century, this historic building is in the Renaissance style with a Gothic cloister and three naves with Plateresque ornamentation inside. It was restored by the Iberdrola Spain Foundation and currently hosts frequent cultural activities such as English courses for students and teachers in the community or the Classical Theater Festival.

To learn in detail about this monument and its more than five centuries of history, they organize free guided tours thanks to the Iberdrola foundation.
Driving along the EX-117 road between Villa del Rey and Alcántara you can see the old bridge that seems to have been submerged by the creation of the dam on the Jartin river.

Villa del Rey is a Spanish municipality located in the province of Cáceres, in the autonomous community of Extremadura. Its orography is characterized by wide plains with gentle slopes, and the entire municipality is contained by the hydrographic basin of the Tagus River, with the Jartín River being its main aquifer.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Villa del Rey from Mata de Alcántara – Circular by Alcántara and the rivers Tagus and Jartín route is approximately 27.2 kilometers long. Most touring cyclists can expect to complete it in about 1 hour and 44 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features an elevation gain and loss of around 215 meters. It is largely composed of paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring bicycles. While generally accessible, a good level of fitness is recommended to comfortably enjoy the ride.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Mata de Alcántara. While specific parking facilities aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the village of Mata de Alcántara to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of natural landscapes and historical points of interest. You'll pass by the Church of San Andrés in Mata de Alcántara, the historic Conventual de San Benito de Alcántara, the Old Bridge of the Jartín River, and the Church of Santiago of Villa del Rey. The scenery also includes views of the Tagus and Jartín rivers and the characteristic Extremaduran landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region can experience hot summers. For the most comfortable cycling experience, consider visiting in the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo, Ruta del Balcón del Mundo, Camino de la Alberquita, Camino de los Huertos, Camino de los Sanjuanes, Barriada del Puente Romano, Camino de Villa del Rey a Navas del Madroño, and Camino de la Media Luna.

faqs.question: Is the Villa del Rey from Mata de Alcántara – Circular by Alcántara and the rivers Tagus and Jartín route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is outdoors and generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails can vary. As a general rule, if you bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept on a leash, and that you clean up after them. Always respect local signage regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Tagus International Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Tagus International Natural Park. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing the natural park for activities like cycling. However, always be mindful of any specific regulations or protected areas within the park that might require special permits for certain activities or access. It's always a good idea to check the official park website for the latest information.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route or nearby?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Mata de Alcántara and Alcántara, both of which are villages where you can find local cafes, bars, and restaurants to refuel or grab a drink. Alcántara, in particular, offers more options due to its historical significance.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the route is considered 'moderate' due to its length and elevation changes, it is largely on paved surfaces. It's suitable for touring cyclists of all skill levels, but having a good level of fitness will make the 27.2-kilometer ride more enjoyable.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike (including a spare tube and pump), sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling attire. A helmet is always advised for safety.
